xml.message: “URGENTTenno,Please meet me at the Necralisk as soon as you are able. This cannot wait.Discretion is paramount. Tell nobody.Loid”

sp.speaker: Loid
sp.speech: Ah! My lonely vigil is over. Follow me, Tenno, and on no account wake the family! Despite their conflicts they sleep in peace, but a solitary dream plagues me.

sp.speaker: Loid
sp.speech: You must understand: constructs CANNOT dream. And yet, I have.

sp.speaker: Loid
sp.speech: In my dream, one single name repeats. KALYMOS

sp.speaker: Loid
sp.speech: The memories surface, long locked away. This was no dream, Tenno. It was a precept. The Kalymos Sequence.

sp.speaker: Loid
sp.speech: I must fulfill this dormant precept. The Kalymos Sequence has begun, and it cannot be undone.

sp.speaker: Loid
sp.speech: I have one responsibility in the Kalymos Sequence: Awaken the Sleeper. But I cannot do it alone. Please, assist me once more?

sp.speaker: Loid
sp.speech: I fear we are off the edge of the map, and into the domain of monsters. Master Entrati never completed his final project: the VESSELS. The Sequence dictates they cannot awaken before the Sleeper does.

sp.speaker: Loid
sp.speech: Do you hear that? Let us hope The Sleeper is untroubled by such... strangeness. Find them.

sp.speaker: Loid
sp.speech: Necramechs. Poor, abandoned things. Still faithfully following Master Entrati's last command, to defend. Show mercy, Tenno. End them.

sp.speaker: Loid
sp.speech: Master Entrati called it the Voidtongue. He feared it was meaningless... and feared more that it was not.

sp.speaker: Loid
sp.speech: There! That book was like a diary to Master Entrati, his grimoire! No doubt it holds the secrets of the Vessels, and of the Kalymos Sequence! Take it, Tenno!

sp.speaker: Loid
sp.speech: The grimoire is very... active. It is... reading ... you!

sp.speaker: Loid
sp.speech: I confess it galls me slightly to see a compendium of knowledge used as a weapon, but needs must. We need be in no doubt of your skill, at any rate.

sp.speaker: Loid
sp.speech: A monstrous danger, these labs. Better off sealed. We can only hope The Sleeper has been untouched by this vast horror. This great Indifference.

sp.speaker: Loid
sp.speech: I ask too much of you, Tenno. These horrors must be stopped!

sp.speaker: Loid
sp.speech: If these fragments were to unite... Destroy them swiftly, Tenno!

sp.speaker: Loid
sp.speech: Right. Onward.

sp.speaker: Automated Announcer
sp.speech: Subway chimesThe next train is arriving. Doors are on the right.

sp.speaker: Loid
sp.speech: A trap? Master Entrati jealously protected his most adored possessions - be they objects... or people. Come. The Sleeper awaits.
The Kalymos Sequence has begun? Did he send you? Is he... No. No, of course he didn't. That would be impossible.

sp.speaker: Loid
sp.speech: This is, well, Loid. The original. All emotional centers very much intact. Did you have a good sleep?

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: Ah. The 'helpful' Necraloid my Albrecht had made, so that I might have assistance... and company. Perhaps that made it... easier for him to leave.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: Resume your duties, construct.

sp.speaker: Loid
sp.speech: Resume your duties, construct.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: Please, follow me, uh...

sp.speaker: Loid
sp.speech: This is a Tenno. An orphan of the Void, continuing the struggle our Master abandoned.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: A Tenno? A deserter, presumably? Is it not aware there's a war going on? *sigh* So. The war, too, is just a memory now.

sp.speaker: Loid
sp.speech: Tenno, while Loid completes his inspections, you'd better meet the Cavia... a reminder of darker times. Though Master Entrati always hoped the awaited Operator would be able to understand them?

sp.speaker: Fibonacci
sp.speech: Yara mig!

sp.speaker: Bird 3
sp.speech: Zek? Aba! Zek? Zek?

sp.speaker: Loid
sp.speech: Ugh, an... incomplete experiment, one might say.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: Albrecht left a great deal... incomplete. The Vessels are unfinished, like a book one might one day return to. Operable, barely. The Sequence dictates I am to wait for their Operator... Clearly, that cannot be you?

sp.speaker: Loid
sp.speech: Oh, it can be. Tenno, Master Entrati's Grimoire suggests you are the key. That colorless antique there - use it.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: This isn't right. The Transference stream... it's being diverted!

sp.speaker: Automated Announcer
sp.speech: Please note: payment processing systems are down. All ticketing stations are cash only.

sp.speaker: Aoi
sp.speech: breathing heavilyArthur. Find a terminal. Check-in. I need to know you made it.

sp.speaker: Arthur
sp.speech: Aoi? Feels like there's... someone in my head...

sp.speaker: Aoi
sp.speech: breathing heavilyNearly done with this bitch of a year. I wish we could just start over. Find that Dr. Entrati bastard on day one. Then I wouldn't be alone on New Year's Eve.

sp.speaker: Aoi
sp.speech: They've found me. Keep going, Arthur. Find Dr Entrati. Follow him into hell if you have to! *uses Polarize*Screaming

sp.speaker: TV Announcer
sp.speech: It's comin' up to midnight, folks! Is YOUR PC ready for the new millennium? Orrrr... are YOU one of the switched-on thinkers who can tell this so-called Y2K event is just a bunch of bullpucky? Stay tuned. Here's where we find out!

sp.speaker: Albrecht Entrati?
sp.speech: You. Are. Late.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: So... Albrecht made it to 1999 in one piece. But that thing is still on his heels. Albrecht rarely smiled, after his return from the Void. And never like that... I smashed the casket for NOTHING! IT FOLLOWED HIM ANYWAY!

sp.speaker: Fibonacci
sp.speech: YARA MIG! URU URU YARA MIG!

sp.speaker: Tagfer
sp.speech: ZEK! ZEK! ABA? ZEK! ZEK! ABA?

sp.speaker: Bird 3
sp.speech: MIG? MIG? RE-UN?

sp.speaker: Loid Original
Loid Original
sp.speech: The Murmur. They're attacking the sensory network I installed, our eyes and ears! To arms, Tenno - play the warrior, and leave the tactics to me!

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: Construct - I'm awake. Your one responsibility is fulfilled. Be with the family. They must be kept at the state of ignorance to which my Albrecht so wisely consigned them.

sp.speaker: Loid
sp.speech: But I - Yes. I see. Until we meet again, Tenno.

sp.speaker: The Man in the Wall
sp.speech: I'd crawl away in shame too if I'd done what LOID did. Doesn't that Grimoire feel a little - light?

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: The Murmur presumes to destroy our auditory and visual sensors. In all the time I have been asleep, they have never ONCE been so bold!

sp.speaker: Tagfer
sp.speech: IIRE! SHOGRUN UKUL!

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: Ignore the Cavia, Tenno. If Albrecht ever understood their babble, he never bothered to share that knowledge with me.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: Here they come. After all those years of creeping, silent encroachment... something has changed them. Stirred them up.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: The Necramechs could never be compelled to attack the Murmur. Albrecht suspected they could no longer distinguish between the Indifference... and himself. A theory he was reluctant to confirm.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: There is a defense system, of sorts. Gather enough of the floating Vosphene Glyphs, and they should reinvigorate the organs you're here to protect. A fallback system, in case... the promised Operator never showed.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: They're shifting focus! Get to the Vitreum! Look at them. 'The Indifference.' He could have given lessons in indifference.

sp.speaker: Fibonacci
sp.speech: KOMEK! UTEH KOMEK!

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: Albrecht once thought the Cavia should have been destroyed. Failures, all. But he briefly showed reluctance, and I saw no reason to punish them for his negligence.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: I saw off Granum's thugs. I beat back the Infested. And by the Void, I shall see the end of these monstrosities.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: Dust yourself down, Tenno. We are still alive. Rejoin me, and the Kalymos Sequence may proceed.

sp.speaker: Fibonacci
sp.speech: YARA MIG! URU URU YARA MIG!

sp.speaker: The Man in the Wall
sp.speech: You're right, Fish. Loid could never figure things out. We have nothing to worry about.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: If the Cavia have been babbling our secrets to the Murmur, I shall immolate the lot of them, promise or no promise.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: It seems Albrecht said more than we heard. Despite the signal becoming corrupted, his words to you are preserved in the Transference buffer. We should extract them... but for that the Pom-2 requires a replacement 'board', and those are all hidden away in the Netracells. You do understand that accessing a Netracell without a Keyglyph is completely impossible?

sp.speaker: Tagfer
sp.speech: Mara lohk! Mara lohk!

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: A Keyglyph. So. The Cavia have provided the very item we require. Very well: proceed to the Netracell at your own risk, Tenno. That babbling oddity may be sending you into a trap, and if you do end up in one, I'm afraid you are on your own.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: Keyglyphs are a heavy burden for living beings to bear. Each type will impede you in its own way.

sp.speaker: The Man in the Wall
sp.speech: laughsLook at these things. Lying around. ABANDONED. Like you, Loid.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: You are his demon! Not mine! I WON'T listen!

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: My apologies, Tenno. I shall refrain from further outbursts.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: There. A Netracell. Albrecht stored all manner of sentimental old junk in these. Insert the Keyglyph.

xml.info: Eliminate Enemies to Lower Security Level

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: The Netracell's security apparatus is diligent. That door won't open while it can sense The Murmur nearby. So. Eliminate them.

xml.info: Find and Destroy the Necramite Drone to Continue

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: Necramites! The lab's automated systems are trying to lock down the Netracell. Your only option is to destroy them.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: Cold stone. Sardonic aloofness. I was a fool not to see the resemblance before.

sp.speaker: The Man in the Wall
sp.speech: You shoulda left well enough alone, kiddo! It'll NEVER get BETTER if you KEEP ON. PICKING. AT. IT!

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: Excellent. Return to me with the board, Tenno.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: Thank you. A moment, please.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: Would you do the honors?

sp.speaker: Albrecht Entrati
sp.speech: Arthur? Tenno? Forgive me. I need Loid to understand why I had to leave. Without him. Why I forced him to destroy the device after I had gone. And why I could never say the words he so needed to hear.

sp.speaker: Albrecht Entrati
sp.speech: If I had remained in the Deimos laboratory, Loid would never have been safe from ... Him. You know of what I speak. And so I retreated to the only place in history where that entity could not easily follow. To draw His greedy eye away from Loid.

sp.speaker: Albrecht Entrati
sp.speech: Tenno - I. Require. Your. Trust. Craft the little curio whose blueprints you will find in the computer's memory. Complete the Kalymos Sequence. Repair. Rebuild. When the time is right, find me.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: The skin of a martyr doesn't suit you, Albrecht. If something mattered... he'd have found a way. It's too late. You heard him, Tenno! He requires your trust. So craft the bloody thing.

sp.speaker: The Man in the Wall
sp.speech: Tick tock, kiddo! You don't wanna be late again, do you?

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: The Jahu Gargoyle. Bring it back here and we shall... examine what 'Form' will do to help us.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: Albrecht may have told you to build that thing, but given how he used people and threw them away, he may not have cared for your safety. If that gargoyle is a threat you must take it from the Sanctum immediately. Now, activate it.
AT LAST. IF IGNORANCE IS BLISS, THEN YOU, TENNO, MUST BE ECSTATIC. COME ON THEN. BRING IT CLOSER! WAKEY WAKEY!

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: Where did that come from? Identify yourself!
PLEASE, LOID, LET THEM FIGURE IT OUT.
THAT'S IT! ALMOST THERE.
YES! YES! VERY GOOD. KEEP GOING.
THAT'S THE RIGHT DIRECTION, OH-HO. WHO'S A GOOD BIPED?
THAT'S THE RIGHT DIRECTION. WHO'S A-A CLEVER LITTLE AIR-BREATHER? YOU ARE. YES, YOU ARE.
NO. THIS WAY.
NO! ARGH! WE'RE GOING TO BE HERE ALL DAY, AREN'T WE?
OH IT NOW IT'S WANDERING OFF AGAIN. HELLOOO?! WRONG! WAY! UGH. BIPEDS.
OH! BY ALL MEANS, STAND AROUND CATCHING SNOWFLAKES ON YOUR TONGUE, IT'S NOT LIKE WE HAVE AN UNFOLDING CRISIS OR ANYTHING!
HELLO?! WAKE UP, TENNO! YOU NEED TO FIND ME!

sp.speaker: Fibonacci
sp.speech: Here! In the jar!
Wha? The fish with the big brain?
YES THE FISH WITH THE BIG BRAIN! Can we PLEASE, finally, get this INTERMINABLE process over with?

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: Albrecht used you to give the Cavia a voice, just as he used me to keep them alive. Such a resourceful man.

sp.speaker: Fibonacci
sp.speech: So here we are then: the best of what's left. Clearly Papa's plan was countered. Color me surprised. Right, then. We may proceed with the Sequence.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: You animals have observed us all this time, have you not? You've given yourself a ROLE in the Sequence?

sp.speaker: Fibonacci
sp.speech: Indeed we have - come, Loid, it's named after Papa's favored pet! Spared the rod and spoiled. We know the Fragmented Ones are heralds of the Indifference. We know the Chosen Operator must summon and destroy them. And regrettably, you, Loid, have made this impossible.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: Choose your words carefully, fish.

sp.speaker: Fibonacci
sp.speech: Tenno: the Grimoire is incomplete, the final page hidden away. By Loid. Eh, be a darling and retrieve it. That's all. Run along.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: So be it - the gift of decency was clearly not bestowed upon the Cavia, Tenno, but I cannot deny the page's current status is lost.

sp.speaker: Bird 3
sp.speech: Talk?
Talk! I can talk! Hey! Hey! Ooh! Ooh! Mister Loid! Mister Loid! Where is Papa? Why did Papa leave us? Mister Loid?

sp.speaker: Tagfer
sp.speech: Papa is gone. We could all be gone too. In an instant. Like... like... We have to keep quiet.

sp.speaker: Bird 3
sp.speech: Okay!

sp.speaker: Fibonacci
sp.speech: Allow me to boil this down for you: should the Indifference's corrupting touch reach us and take back our speech? We would be screwed! So: be a love and eradicate the Murmur in this patch before the big bad gets word that Loid's awake and the fish is talking.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: Tenno... there is some truth to what the... fish... says. That page was the last thing my Albrecht ever wrote. Meant for me. Since I had no interest in reading his endless self-justifications, I hid it away instead. But with all this chaos, it's undetectable.

sp.speaker: Fibonacci
sp.speech: So long as the Indifference is missing a digit, it is confined to the strands of Khra. Don't ask, your tiny mind would likely pop under the strain of understanding. This limitation is our one hope

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: Do not patronize the Tenno, fish. The strands of Khra are merely Void-renderings of the chains of cause and effect. Conceptually embodied timelines, if you will.

sp.speaker: Fibonacci
sp.speech: Papa's daughter excelled at explaining complicated concepts in childish terms. I see now where she learned it.

sp.speaker: Fibonacci
sp.speech: Arousing as I'm sure this is for you, the rest of us are moments away from an extinction Grim! Black! And FINAL! *sigh* So. If you would be so kind. Finish up. So I can point you. Toward. That. Sodding. PAGE!

sp.speaker: Fibonacci
sp.speech: Bravo. It was a long walk toward competence, but here you are. Now, please, the page.

sp.speaker: The Man in the Wall
sp.speech: How about you give me the page instead, Kiddo? Consider it part of our deal.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: There. It's done. Keep it to yourself, Tenno. I'm damned if I'm giving Albrecht the last word.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: Tenno...

sp.speaker: Fibonacci
sp.speech: A fell hand raps, uh, upon the Untime Door, and sans Grimoire we are undone! We've been rumbled.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: Tenno? Are you there?! Thank Void! Listen. The Murmur diverted your Extraction-jump. We need you back at the Sanctum, now! Hurry!

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: It takes and it takes! It took my future, it's taking the Labs inch by inch... well, I won't let it take his BLOODY Cavia!

sp.speaker: Fibonacci
sp.speech: The sodding Indifference seeks a path through the walls, and then, Tenno, through you! Keep it at bay!

sp.speaker: The Man in the Wall
sp.speech: VOULL NE XATA VOK, MARA LOHK?

sp.speaker: Fibonacci
sp.speech: Rrrr. *groan* The strand holds. The Indifference approaches! Soon... it will take on mass.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: Get out! There is nothing for you here! Get out! OUT!!

sp.speaker: Fibonacci
sp.speech: Tenno, the Grimoire must speak with its own voice!

xml.info: Connect the Grimoire to the Gargoyle

sp.speaker: Albrecht Entrati
sp.speech: Loid... You gave me back my sight, but I would not spare you a glance. You gave me back my voice, but I never spoke to praise you as I should have. For you were my right hand. My strength, my support, my sanctuary. Even when the galleries hurled scorn upon me, your sole desire was to suffer it in my stead.

sp.speaker: Albrecht Entrati
sp.speech: On your first day of service, you aspired to be worthy of me. I only wish that I could have been worthy of you, my Loid.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: This stillness... Tenno... No. Operator.

sp.speaker: Fibonacci
sp.speech: The strand of Khra has been cut while the Indifference remains undeterred. I foresee a grim and continuing struggle to safeguard Papa's laboratory from its fell and ruinous grasp.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: Indeed. I shall not waver.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: 'Protect the Vessels. Repair. Rebuild.' So runs the Kalymos Sequence, and such is our task. In the version of the past we saw, My Albrecht said we were too late. The solution, naturally, is to arrive on time, next time. You keep the Murmur at bay, I shall fulfill my role: Punctuality. The Entrati family never could arrive on time without my help. And so... here I am, back to my old role. The diligent servant. Albrecht would have smiled at that, I think.

xml.info: What did he say to you, right before he left?

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: He said...

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: ...do not let it see you weeping, Loid.

xml.info: ...that this was no way to remember me.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: ...my last sight of your face must not be marred by tears.

sp.speaker: Loid Original
sp.speech: ...how do I still deserve your tears after all I have done?

sp.speaker: The Man in the Wall
sp.speech: We had a deal. The deal was shook. A little handshake, all it took! Though from the deal the wee child ran...

sp.speaker: Albrecht Entrati?
sp.speech: Yet still... WE END AS WE BEGAN.

xml.message: “An Appropriate Technique?Chosen Operator. A friendly suggestion: you could do so much more with those Melee weapons of yours. I donate my wisdom on both tool and technique. Listen well.TENNOKAI, a fighting technique fusing Void and Beastial instinct. Raw power. While I have never used Tennokai, I understand it perfectly. Somewhat of a mind mastery. Enough so that lost materials pertaining to Tennokai have been stored just out of my reach in the Sanctum.Tools. Papa was not one to rest, use this segment to unlock more potential for your Arsenal. The Arcane was an art and science that Papa studied well - the convexity of my bowl and a magnifying glass on his research, and now your gain.Install this segment, acquire Melee Arcanes, find Tennokai Modules, and observe the results: the swift deletion of your foes.”

“For the TennoTenno, the Warframe Qorvex was designed by my Albrecht's own hand, to protect the chosen Operator from the hazards of his laboratory. This design is yours by right.I intend to keep the promise I made to him. When the time is right and the equipment repaired, I will assist you to travel back to the past, the pivotal year of 1999.Perhaps, between us, we may change history.Loid”
